name: Arifa Ghani Rahman
title: Head & Associate Professor
bio_raw:
Arifa Ghani Rahman has almost thirty years of experience in teaching literature and writing in Bangladeshi and US universities. In addition, she is an editor and translator with extensive experience. She has published translations of several short stories, a book of poetry, a novel, and an autobiography. She has also translated another autobiography that is awaiting publication. Arifa is the Executive Editor of
Crossings: A Journal of English Studies,
the peer-reviewed journal of the Department of English and Humanities, and the former Coordinator of the MA in English program. She is the Faculty Trainer for Moodle, the learning management software used at ULAB. Arifa also served as the Advisor to the Communications Office at ULAB from 2020-2022.
Arifa has been teaching face-to-face at Rivier University, New Hampshire, USA since 2002, hybrid since 2006, and online from Bangladesh since 2013. She has a BA and MA in English from the University of Dhaka, Bangladesh where she was awarded a gold medal for her Masters result. Following a three-year teaching career at Jahangirnagar University, Bangladesh, she completed another MA in English degree from Texas A&M University, College Station, USA. Currently, she is the Head of the Department of English and Humanities. She teaches fiction, nonfiction, and research at ULAB. Born in Bangladesh, Arifa grew up in Nigeria and spent a good part of her life in the US. At this point, she likes to boast that she has spent approximately each decade of her life on a different continent.
stated_interests: Fiction, Nonfiction, Bangladeshi Women's Writing, Research and Writing, Online Learning, Editing and Translation
